reasoning: The spot exchange rate dropped precipitously from 1.422 (April 7) to 1.3895 (April 11). According to the FRED trend analysis, a linear fit predicts a rebound toward 1.406 by mid-April, suggesting this sharp drop leaves the pair oversold in the short term. The Bank of Canada meets on April 16 and continues to face economic headwinds; markets price a 45% chance of a 25-bps rate cut, which would weaken CAD and push USD/CAD upwards. We assign a higher probability (0.60) to the rate exceeding 1.3895 by April 20. Over longer horizons, experts forecast broader US dollar weakness into late 2025 due to expected Federal Reserve rate cuts and easing macroeconomic dominance. In accordance with forecasting principles, uncertainty increases over longer timelines, meaning predictions should revert toward 0.5. The probabilities smoothly decay to 0.52 by October as the long-term US dollar depreciation trend neutralizes the near-term oversold conditions.
